# FinOps Framework 2026 — Capabilities by Domain

> Source: [finops.org/framework/domains](https://www.finops.org/framework/domains/)

All four domains operate in parallel, not sequentially. Capabilities are interdependent across domains.

---

## Domain 1: Understand Usage & Cost (4 capabilities)

Gathering comprehensive data about technology utilization and expenses.

| # | Capability | Change |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Data Ingestion | — |
| 2 | Allocation | — |
| 3 | Reporting & Analytics | — |
| 4 | Anomaly Management | — |

---

## Domain 2: Quantify Business Value (5 capabilities)

Connecting usage and cost information with organizational value, ensuring transparency around technology investments.

| # | Capability | Change |
|---|---|---|
| 5 | Planning & Estimating | — |
| 6 | Forecasting | — |
| 7 | Budgeting | — |
| 8 | KPIs & Benchmarking | Renamed from "Benchmarking" |
| 9 | Unit Economics | — |

---

## Domain 3: Optimize Usage & Cost (5 capabilities)

Ensuring resources deliver value through efficient consumption and favorable pricing arrangements.

| # | Capability | Change |
|---|---|---|
| 10 | Architecting & Workload Placement | Renamed from "Architecting for Cloud" + merged "Onboarding Workloads" |
| 11 | Usage Optimization | Renamed from "Workload Optimization" |
| 12 | Rate Optimization | — |
| 13 | Licensing & SaaS | — |
| 14 | Sustainability | Renamed from "Cloud Sustainability" |

---

## Domain 4: Manage the FinOps Practice (8 capabilities)

Enabling continuous advancement by aligning people, processes, and technology throughout the organization.

| # | Capability | Change |
|---|---|---|
| 15 | Executive Strategy Alignment | **NEW** |
| 16 | FinOps Practice Operations | — |
| 17 | Governance, Policy & Risk | Renamed from "Policy & Governance" |
| 18 | FinOps Education & Enablement | — |
| 19 | Invoicing & Chargeback | — |
| 20 | FinOps Assessment | — |
| 21 | Automation, Tools & Services | Renamed from "FinOps Tools & Services" |
| 22 | Intersecting Disciplines | Enhanced content |

---

## Executive Strategy Alignment (Detail)

New capability connecting technology spend, usage, and adoption to business strategy and priorities. Helps leaders compare options, make tradeoffs, and govern investment for value.

**Four sub-areas:**

1. **Executive Priority Alignment** — Relates spending patterns to strategic initiatives, strengthens forecasting with unit economics (cost per token, cost per transaction), embeds cost accountability into performance structures
2. **Multi-Year Investment Strategy** — Forecasts vendor commitments across cloud, SaaS, AI, and data center; manages overlapping legacy and transition costs during modernization
3. **Facilitate Product Prioritization Strategy** — Integrated cost visibility and TCO analysis across the technology portfolio; supports early-stage cost estimation during architecture design
4. **Enable Strategic Decision Support** — Governance structures, decision rights, spend guardrails, decision continuity through organizational changes (M&A, leadership transitions)

**Target audience**: Executive leaders (VP, SVP, EVP level) and FinOps teams supporting strategic decision-making.

---

## Architecting & Workload Placement (Detail)

Designing and modernizing solutions with cost awareness and efficiency. Encompasses both the former "Architecting for Cloud" and "Onboarding Workloads" capabilities.

**Seven functional areas:**
1. Strategy Management — governance touchpoints for workload decisions
2. Workload Evaluation — identify candidates from new demand and existing workloads
3. Design Understanding — cost-effective architectures across technology categories
4. Business Case Development — financial comparisons between options
5. Implementation Planning — sequencing, dependencies, transitions
6. Execution Coordination — foundations and cost transparency during implementation
7. Continuous Review — post-change evaluation and lessons capture

---

## Usage Optimization (Detail)

Ensures resources across all FinOps Scopes are properly selected, correctly sized, only run when needed, appropriately configured, and highly utilized. Consolidates former "Workload Management & Automation" and "Resource Utilization & Efficiency" concepts.

Applies across all technology categories, not just cloud infrastructure.

---

## Intersecting Disciplines (Enhanced)

Coordination with allied disciplines across the technology organization:
- **ITAM** (IT Asset Management) — asset lifecycle, license compliance
- **SAM** (Software Asset Management) — software licensing optimization
- **ITFM** (IT Financial Management) — budget alignment, cost allocation
- **Security** — cost of security controls, compliance spend
- **Sustainability** — carbon/energy cost of technology
- **Enterprise Architecture** — workload placement decisions
